novel_features.name: Certificate fit finder
novel_features.description: Find available Hyatt award nights that fit a Cat 1-4 or Cat 1-7 certificate before it expires.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining local hotel category metadata, synced award calendar rows, and certificate constraints.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the user needs to burn a Hyatt certificate without manually checking category and date fit.

novel_features.name: Flexible density matrix
novel_features.description: Show which dates or weeks have the most Hyatt award options across a hotel shortlist.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregating local calendar-day rows across multiple hotels and date buckets.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before picking travel dates when flexibility matters more than a specific property.

novel_features.name: Split-stay builder
novel_features.description: Build viable multi-hotel Hyatt itineraries when no one property has the full stay.
novel_features.rationale: Requires solving contiguous nightly coverage across multiple local hotel calendars.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the user will switch hotels to make an award trip work.

novel_features.name: Watch volatility ranking
novel_features.description: Rank watched Hyatt hotels by how often award space opens, closes, or changes price.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local watch snapshot history and delta aggregation that Hyatt's page does not expose.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to decide which hard-to-book Hyatt searches deserve closer monitoring.

novel_features.name: Off-peak opportunity finder
novel_features.description: Find off-peak or unusually low-point Hyatt award clusters across synced calendar data.
novel_features.rationale: Requires comparing local pointsLevel and pointsValue rows across hotels, categories, and dates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the user wants low-points timing rather than a specific hotel.

novel_features.name: Room-category ladder
novel_features.description: Compare standard, club, and suite award availability side by side for one Hyatt stay window.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aligning cached calendar rows across multiple Hyatt roomCategory values for the same hotel and dates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the user cares whether better room categories are open, not just standard rooms.

novel_features.name: Snapshot coverage audit
novel_features.description: Show stale, missing, or uneven local Hyatt calendar coverage before trusting a scan.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local snapshot metadata and requested hotel/date windows.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when browser protection or expired cookies may have left gaps in cached availability.

narrative.headline: Hyatt award availability as a scriptable, local, agent-readable CLI.
narrative.value_prop: Hyatt's calendar is useful but browser-bound and property-by-property. This CLI resolves cities into Hyatt hotels and spirit codes, turns points-calendar pages into structured local data, and separates standard-room availability from other room categories. It also treats length of stay as an availability-changing input, so one-night results are never reused as proof of multi-night award space.
narrative.auth_narrative: The first shippable flow uses public Hyatt Points Calendar pages and browser-compatible replay. Logged-in Hyatt sessions are short-lived, so account-specific perks or durable authenticated availability are intentionally out of initial scope.

quickstart.comment: Check local configuration and transport assumptions without touching Hyatt.
Fetch one browser-sniffed Points Calendar surface and keep only fields an agent needs.
Preview Hyatt property metadata hydration before building the local hotel index.

quickstart.command: hyatt-cli hotels resolve-city --city "New York City" --json --select name,spiritCode,city,state,category
quickstart.comment: Turn a city into the matching Hyatt hotels and the spirit codes used by availability scans.

quickstart.command: hyatt-cli scan city --city "New York City" --start 2026-09-01 --end 2026-09-07 --nights 2 --room-categories STANDARD_ROOM,SUITE --agent
quickstart.comment: Scan every matching city hotel and label standard-room versus other room-category award space.
Find flexible-date award clusters across a shortlist.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `hyatt-cli doctor hyatt` and refresh the browser-cookie path before scanning again.

troubleshoots.symptom: Scan results look sparse or uneven.
troubleshoots.fix: Run `hyatt-cli awards coverage --hotels <codes> --start <date> --end <date>` to identify missing cached months or room categories.
Treat logged-in state as temporary; rerun the public calendar flow or use a fresh browser profile capture.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when checking World of Hyatt points availability across properties, dates, room categories, or watchlists. It is best for repeatable award-search workflows where local snapshots, structured output, and agent-readable filters matter.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to make or cancel Hyatt reservations.
Do not use this CLI for durable logged-in account benefits or elite-status planning.
Do not use this CLI as a replacement for Hyatt customer service when availability or booking rules are disputed.

recipes.title: Check one Points Calendar page
recipes.explanation: Fetch one Hyatt standard-room calendar page and narrow the nested output to a specific award row.

recipes.title: Resolve a city into Hyatt hotel codes
recipes.command: hyatt-cli hotels resolve-city --city "New York City" --agent --select name,spiritCode,city,state,category
recipes.explanation: Find the hotels and spirit codes to use when the user only knows the city.

recipes.title: Scan a city by room category
recipes.command: hyatt-cli scan city --city "New York City" --start 2026-09-01 --end 2026-09-07 --nights 2 --room-categories STANDARD_ROOM,SUITE --agent --select hotelName,spiritCode,date,nights,roomCategory,isStandardRoom,pointsValue
recipes.explanation: Search every matching city hotel and keep room type plus length of stay explicit in the output.

recipes.title: Find certificate-eligible awards
recipes.explanation: Combine local hotel categories and synced calendar rows to find certificate-fit nights.

recipes.title: Compare flexible weekly availability
recipes.explanation: See which weeks have the most two-night award options across a hotel shortlist.

recipes.title: Build a split-stay option
recipes.explanation: Find a viable award itinerary across hotels when one property does not have all nights.

trigger_phrases: Hyatt hotels in New York City with points
search Hyatt award rooms by city

gaps: Direct HTTP to Hyatt returned bot-protection responses in discovery; runtime must expose diagnostics and browser-cookie guidance.
Logged-in Hyatt sessions are short-lived and account-specific flows are out of initial scope.
patterns: HTML page with embedded JavaScript state
Local SQLite snapshots for flexible-date and watchlist commands
Length-of-stay-aware cache keys and availability output
